title: The Verification Step
concept: The large model accepts or rejects the draft tokens based on probability alignment; accepted tokens are generated instantly, skipping multiple large forward passes.

exercises.prompt: Who decides if the draft tokens are accepted or rejected?
exercises.explain: In speculative decoding, the large model has the final say. It verifies whether the draft tokens are good enough to keep.

cards.title: The Double-Check Secret
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how AI can write super fast without losing its smarts? It uses a clever trick: a smaller AI guesses the words, and a larger AI quickly double-checks them all at once!

cards.title: What is Verification?
cards.body: The verification step is where the large, smart AI model checks the draft tokens suggested by the smaller, faster model. It compares their probability and decides which ones to accept and where to correct them, all in a single run.

cards.title: The Editor and the Typist
cards.body: Imagine a professional author (the large model) working with a fast typist (the drafting model). The typist writes a whole sentence quickly. The author reads it and says, "Yes, yes, yes, NO—change that last word." The author keeps the good parts instantly without having to type them all out from scratch!

cards.title: Draft Verification in Action
cards.body: If the small model drafts "The sky is bright and blue", the large model checks this sequence. If it agrees with "The sky is bright and", those 5 tokens are saved instantly. If it prefers "sunny" instead of "blue", it stops there, accepts the first 5, and updates the last one.

cards.title: Myth: Checking takes too long!
cards.body: Many think that verifying draft tokens takes just as long as generating them normally. In reality, the large model can check multiple draft tokens in a single step, saving time by avoiding individual calculations for every single word.

cards.title: Verification Speedup
cards.body: See how checking a whole block of text at once compares to generating it step-by-step.

right.text: The small model drafts ahead, and the large model checks and accepts them in one quick go.

left.text: The large model runs a full calculation for every single token, taking much longer.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of verifying draft tokens in a single forward pass?

options.text: It saves time by processing multiple tokens at once instead of one-by-one.
It makes the small model smarter than the large model.
It trains the large model to write shorter sentences.
It eliminates the need for any large AI models.

exercises.explain: By checking draft tokens in a single batch, the large model avoids having to run its full calculation process step-by-step for every single word.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The large model must reject all draft tokens if even one word is slightly off.
exercises.explain: The verification step allows the large model to accept or reject draft tokens based on probability, keeping the high-quality outputs.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the description of speculative decoding:
exercises.sentence: The large model [blank] the draft tokens all at once to speed up generation.
exercises.explain: The large model acts as the validator, verifying the draft tokens to make sure they match what it would have written.

exercises.prompt: Which AI behavior represents the verification step?

options.text: An AI model that writes a sentence word-by-word very slowly.
An AI model that looks at a 5-word draft, accepts the first 3 words, and rewrites the 4th word.
An AI model that randomly deletes half of its generated text.

exercises.explain: AI model B is acting as the verifier in speculative decoding, checking the draft and correcting the first mistake it finds.

exercises.prompt: How does the Verification Step actually speed up text generation?

options.text: It deletes the draft and makes the large model rewrite everything from scratch.
It verifies the draft batch in one go, keeping correct tokens and correcting the first mistake.
It slows down the process by making both models guess the same words.
It translates the draft tokens into a different language for safety.

exercises.explain: The verification step is the core of speculative decoding. The large model checks a batch of draft tokens at once. Accepted ones are kept instantly, while the first rejected one is corrected. This saves massive amounts of computing time!
